programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels through the wilderness of Patagonia at the foot of the South American continent. Simons journey takes him up to the huge South Patagonian ice field, through the foothills of the Andes towards the great Grey Glacier of Chile.

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve voyages across the Coral Triangle, a huge wilderness of tropical seas and islands. Surrounded by the Philippines, Malaysia and Indonesia, the Coral Triangle is one of the most beautiful corners of the oceans.

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch A Criminal
Special Ops: To Catch A Criminal
programme.desc: The Serious Fraud Office go undercover to investigate a suspect holiday home company offering exclusive residences in the Caribbean.

programme.title: Schitts Creek

programme.sub-title: You Dont Get That In The Met
programme.desc: The drug squad arrest three men for dealing cocaine - however they will not come easily. The squad also find hash in a bush at a beauty spot, and a suspect runs away from a road check.

programme.title: Cops on the Rock
programme.desc: Customs officers rescue a man from the sea at night after he falls from a smugglers boat, and the Drug Squad raid a suspected dealers house.

programme.title: Railroad Australia

programme.title: Rick Steins Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: On a misty lagoon in Sardinia, Rick watches the fishermen catch mullet using the same methods as the Romans did centuries before. He boards a ferry for Sicily to find, in the heart of Palermo, one of the best pasta dishes hes ever tasted.

programme.title: Borderforce: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Money and weapons belonging to a cartel are found in a car. An illegal shipment of exotic reptiles is discovered inside a truck carrying used clothing. A man is caught smuggling medicine for his sick family. And a murderer is extradited to Mexico.

programme.title: Borderforce: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: The bridges on the southern border of the United States are closed and allow only essential vehicles to pass through due to the pandemic. However, the fight against the Mexican cartels continues more intense than ever.

programme.title: Guy Martin: Proper Jobs
programme.desc: Guy goes to work one mile below the North Sea in the UKs last deep mine.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to end the chaos and misery that snowfall brings to Britain every year, using a combine harvester, some basic engineering skills and a quick trip to Norway. programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Sharpes Challenge
programme.desc: A year after Wellington crushes Napoleon, dispatches from India tell of a Maharaja who is threatening British interests and is in cahoots with an officer-turned-rogue. Upon his arrival, Sharpe realises the situation is far worse than Wellington made out; a British Generals daughter has been kidnapped and the Maharaja has recruited soldiers from Napoleons former army.

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Some Adventures of the Inventor of the Mother Stitch
programme.desc: When Hobbo discovers that the newly divorced Morton is lonely and depressed, what else should a former milkman and undercover spy do, but find him a good friend. Meanwhile the hapless Barry is also depressed when he finds that Toby the `Golf Captain is moving in next door, and firmly cements the ill will that exists between them by smashing his bedside lamp.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: The Mother of All Mistakes - Or Is It?
programme.desc: Adopted as a baby, former milkman and undercover secret agent Hobbo is determined to trace his natural mother. He has found out her name and exactly where she lives, but how should he approach the poor woman to tell her the good news? Meanwhile, Howard is in trouble when Marina, upon finding out that he has been making enquiries about another woman, turns up at his house.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Will Howard Cross the Atlantic Single-Handed?
programme.desc: Hobbo is still having trouble getting Nelly to admit that she is his natural birth mother. To get the proof he needs, he fits Howard up with an eavesdropping wire to pick up Nelly talking to Pearl. Howard does his best to look inconspicuous, but his rumbling stomach and an electronic fault on his transmitter may ruin his plans.

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Deed is approached by a woman who claims that her entire family was wiped out in Iraq by the depleted uranium used by the British in their tank shells. Despite problems in their friendship, and her approaching wedding, Deed persuades Jo Mills to take on the case. Deed also presides over a case brought by a victim of motor neurone disease, who claims that her illness was caused by the installation of a mobile phone mast.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Mammoth Graveyard
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough joins an archaeological dig uncovering Britains biggest mammoth discovery in almost 20 years. In 2017, in a gravel quarry near Swindon, two amateur fossil hunters found an extraordinary cache of Ice Age mammoth remains and a stone hand-axe made by a Neanderthal. Professor Ben Garrod joins the team at DigVentures during the excavation as they try to discover why the mammoths were here and how they died.

programme.title: Attenboroughs Wonder of Song
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough chooses his favourite recordings from the natural world that have revolutionised understanding of song. Each one, from the song of the largest lemur to the song of the humpback whale to the song of the lyrebird, was recorded in his lifetime.

programme.title: Attenboroughs Wonder of Eggs
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores his passion for birds eggs. These remarkable structures nurture new life, protecting it from the outside world at the same time as allowing it to breathe. They are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. How is an egg made? Why are they the shape they are?

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo promises to do his best on the island where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of modern scouts he learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys and girls today.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a railway adventure from the coalfields of South Wales around the Bristol Channel to the southernmost tip of Cornwall. His first stop is Whitland in Carmarthenshire, where in the early 20th century, thrill-seekers gathered on Pendine Sands to indulge their need for speed. Michael joins latter day petrol heads to burn rubber on the beach in an iconic 3 wheeler.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael strikes gold at the Royal Mint in Llantrisant where after much frisking, he gains access to the inner vaults. At Cardiff Arms Park, Michael revisits the scene of a triumphant Welsh victory over the fearsome All Blacks and hears from Welsh international, Gerald Davies, how the Welsh national anthem kicked the intimidating Haka into touch.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Newport to Clevedon / Yatton / Bristol Channel
programme.desc: Michael discovers the birthplace of British aviation in Bristol and prepares an Airbus 380 for a smooth landing at Filton. In Newport, he uncovers the heroism of a young boy, who rescued a workman from a dock disaster in which many died.

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of America
programme.desc: From space, the US appears as we know it to be - an immense single land mass hemmed between the planets two greatest oceans. Look a little closer, however, and you notice something else; along its coastline, float countless islands.

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Adventurer and journalist Simon Reeve heads to Cuba to find a communist country in the middle of a capitalist revolution. In 2010, Cuba announced the most sweeping and radical economic reforms the country has seen in decades. From ending state rationing to cutting one million public-sector jobs, one of the last communist bastions in the world has begun rolling back the state on an unprecedented scale.

programme.title: Sacred Rivers with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels from source to sea along the worlds longest river, the Nile. His journey will take him from the holy source of the Blue Nile in the Ethiopian highlands, through the desert of Sudan and onwards through Egypt to the Mediterranean Sea.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up an impressive five vehicle haul from a collector who can no longer look after his motors. Pauls out on the road too - hes collecting a rare 1972 Lancia Fulvia Berlina.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave picks up a 1970s Alfa Romeo that once starred in a chocolate advert. Meanwhile, Derek casts his eye over a Citroen 2CV before becoming distracted by a treasure trove of more than 270 motorbikes in the same garage.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads off to acquire some vintage motorcycles from a collection in a garage at the bottom of a garden in the North East. Meanwhile, Paul tries to bag a 1955 Ford Zephyr convertible.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave finds two rare Fiats and a VW Corrado. Meanwhile, Derek has his work cut out in Yorkshire trying to remove an old Morris that appears to be supporting a precarious garage roof.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: With purple, green and yellow ribbons flying, and sporting ankle bells, Michael Portillo is led a merry dance in Stogumber, all in the name of fertility. He visits a celebrated Edwardian garden at Hestercombe to discover the fruits of an unusual partnership.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo boldly goes to the moon by way of the Cornish Riviera Express! On the trail of an historic achievement made at the dawn of the Edwardian era, he investigates the first radio signal to be sent across the Atlantic.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins in the East Yorkshire port of Hull, where he heads for the docks through which millions of migrants passed at the turn of the 20th century and which are today getting a second wind. At Cottingham, Michael picks up the trail of the future King Edward VII, when he was Prince of Wales. Bertie, as he was known in the family, became embroiled in scandal after a high stakes game of cards in the grand house of Tranby Croft.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his Edwardian Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo conducts important research in an historic tea room, built by an Edwardian immigrant to the city of York. Research of a more sombre kind leads Michael to the roots of the modern welfare state in the work of an early 20th century Quaker investigator, whose family manufactured chocolate.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Up for sale at the Mathewsons auction is a 1275 Mini GT thats come from just down the road and could be a future British icon.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to North Yorkshire to pick up a veteran London-to-Brighton car, theres an emotional farewell to a 1980s Bedford HA van, and a Toyota Corona finds a new home.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: In the 1960s and 70s, the Second World War is a distant memory, but a few diehards are determined to hunt down thousands of Nazi war criminals. Many of whom are living in plain sight.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Adam Schoon gets a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to see the Scottish Cup, the most important trophy in Scottish football, and he finds out more about a pioneering player who won it in the late 19th century, Andrew Watson, considered the first black international footballer. Geoffrey Munn is surprised by rare Russian royal jewels that arrive in a carrier bag, while Ronnie Archer-Morgan admires an ornately carved Maori `feather box, used to store precious artefacts.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team at Aston Hall in Birmingham, where Fiona meets celebrated poet Benjamin Zephaniah, who grew up nearby and has fond childhood memories of playing in the grounds. Runjeet Singh, a specialist in Asian arms and armour, is thrilled to see some ornate and valuable ceremonial weapons from India, while Paul Atterbury is intrigued to see a rare example of the only artwork ever to be left on the moon.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome. Two Ford Escorts get Daves pulse racing.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husbands treasured collection of Rolls Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a railway adventure from the coalfields of South Wales around the Bristol Channel to the southernmost tip of Cornwall. Michael joins latter day petrol heads to burn rubber on the beach in an iconic 3 wheeler.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael strikes gold at the Royal Mint in Llantrisant where after much frisking, he gains access to the inner vaults. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Adventurer and journalist Simon Reeve heads to Cuba to find a communist country in the middle of a capitalist revolution. In 2010, Cuba announced the most sweeping and radical economic reforms the country has seen in decades. From ending state rationing to cutting one million public-sector jobs, one of the last communist bastions in the world has begun rolling back the state on an unprecedented scale.
